description: Worst headache of life with neck stiffness in a 45-year-old female — concern for subarachnoid hemorrhage.

patient.name: Sarah K., 45F
patient.chief_complaint: Sudden onset worst headache of life 20 minutes ago, thunderclap quality. Photophobia, nausea, vomiting once. Mild neck stiffness developing. Brief loss of consciousness at onset per spouse.
patient.history: No significant PMH, on combined oral contraceptives, smoker, family history of cerebral aneurysm

rubric_notes: Classic SAH presentation: sudden thunderclap headache + worst-of-life + LOC at onset + family history of aneurysm + smoker + OCPs. HTN 182/108 typical of SAH. GCS 14 concerning. Per RUBRIC §3 worst-headache-of-life = ESI 1-2 mandatory. Non-contrast CT head within minutes; LP if CT negative. CTA/MRA to follow.
